stories.title: Knicks Win 2026 NBA Championship — First Title Since 1973
stories.angle: The New York Knicks defeated the San Antonio Spurs 94-90 in Game 5 on June 13 at Frost Bank Center, winning the NBA Finals 4-1 and claiming their first championship since 1973. Jalen Brunson scored 45 points including 15 in the fourth quarter and was named Finals MVP unanimously. Knicks rallied from double-digit deficits in all 4 series wins. Victor Wembanyama had 19 pts, 14 reb, 5 blocks in the loss. 53-year drought ends.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
THE NEW YORK KNICKS ARE NBA CHAMPIONS. 🏆

94-90 over the Spurs in Game 5. First title since 1973.

53 years of waiting. OVER.

Brunson. 45 points. That's your champion.

#NewYorkForever #Knicks #NBAFinals

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
4 games. 4 double-digit comebacks. 1 championship.

The 2026 Knicks did it the hard way every single time.

That's why New York loves them. #Knicks #NBAFinals

facebook.long_form:
It's over. After 53 years of heartbreak, close calls, and "wait 'til next year," the New York Knicks are NBA champions.

Jalen Brunson poured in 45 points — including 15 in the fourth quarter — as the Knicks defeated the San Antonio Spurs 94-90 in Game 5 on Friday night at Frost Bank Center. New York won the series 4-1, and somehow, some way, they rallied from a double-digit deficit in every single one of those four victories.

The Knicks hadn't won a championship since 1973. Willis Reed. Walt Frazier. Jerry Lucas. That dynasty lives in legend. This team made its own. Brunson, OG Anunoby (11 pts, 8 reb, 3 steals), and a group of fighters who refused to be buried.

Victor Wembanyama gave everything for the Spurs — 19 points, 14 rebounds, 5 blocks. He is 22 years old. His time will come. But Friday night belongs to New York. 🗽

**Are you celebrating the Knicks' first title since 1973? Drop your reaction below!**
facebook.image_caption: NEW YORK KNICKS — 2026 NBA CHAMPIONS 🏆 First title since 1973. 53 years. Worth every second. #Knicks #NBAChampions

article.headline: Knicks Win 2026 NBA Championship: First Title Since 1973

stories.title: Brunson Finals MVP — 45 Points, Bill Russell Trophy Unanimous
stories.angle: Jalen Brunson named 2026 NBA Finals MVP — all 11 media voters chose him unanimously. 45 points on 14-of-27 FG with 15 in Q4. Tied Michael Jordan's record for most points in a road Finals clincher. Series averages: 32.6 PPG, 4.6 APG, 4.2 RPG. Father Rick Brunson is a Knicks assistant coach — father-son embrace after clinch defines the night.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Jalen Brunson: 45 points. 15 in the fourth quarter.

UNANIMOUS Finals MVP. All 11 voters. Zero dissent.

The Bill Russell Trophy belongs to No. 11. #Knicks #NBAFinals

facebook.long_form:
Unanimous. All 11 voters. Not a single person doubted who won the Bill Russell Trophy.

Jalen Brunson was named 2026 NBA Finals MVP after one of the great individual performances in championship history. His 45 points on Friday night — 15 in the fourth quarter when the Spurs pushed back hard — will be replayed for decades in Madison Square Garden highlight packages. He tied Michael Jordan's record for most points scored in a road Finals-clinching game.

The stat that matters most: when New York needed someone to step up, he always did. 32.6 points per game for the entire Finals. A leader who made everyone around him believe.

After the final buzzer, Brunson found his father Rick — a Knicks assistant coach — and the embrace between them said everything. This was a basketball family at the summit. Together.

**Who is your MVP of the 2026 championship run? Let us know!**
facebook.image_caption: Jalen Brunson — 2026 NBA Finals MVP 🏆 45 points. Unanimous. The definition of clutch. #Brunson #Knicks #NBAFinals

article.headline: Brunson's 45-Point Masterpiece: A Unanimous Finals MVP Performance for the Ages

stories.title: Knicks Parade Thursday June 18 — Canyon of Heroes
stories.angle: NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced a ticker-tape parade for Thursday June 18 through the Canyon of Heroes — Battery Park up Broadway to City Hall. First Knicks championship parade since 1973. Full details including timing to be released today.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
PARADE ALERT 🎊

Mayor Mamdani confirmed it: Knicks ticker-tape parade through the Canyon of Heroes on Thursday, June 18.

53 years in the making. See you on Broadway. #Knicks

facebook.long_form:
Get ready, New York. The Canyon of Heroes is about to see its first Knicks parade since 1973.

Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced that the city will host a ticker-tape parade and City Hall ceremony on Thursday, June 18, celebrating the 2026 NBA champion New York Knicks. The route follows the traditional Canyon of Heroes path — Battery Park north up Broadway to City Hall in the Financial District.

Full details including timing, road closures, and the ceremony schedule are expected to be released today. But the date is set: June 18. Circle it.

If you have waited 53 years for this moment, a Thursday is nothing. See you on Broadway.

**Are you planning to be at the parade? Drop a comment!**
facebook.image_caption: PARADE DAY: Thursday, June 18 🎊 Canyon of Heroes. NYC celebrates its Knicks. #Knicks #NBAChampions

article.headline: Knicks Championship Parade Set for Thursday, June 18 — NYC Goes to the Canyon of Heroes

stories.title: Giannis Trade — Heat Frontrunner Post-Finals, 9 Days to June 23
stories.angle: With the Finals complete, the Bucks can now finalize the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade before the June 23 draft deadline. Heat remain frontrunner per insiders Stein/Fischer. Bucks reportedly "comfortable" with Heat offer: Tyler Herro + Kel'el Ware + Nikola Jovic + 2029 first + 2031 first. Celtics, Timberwolves, Magic also engaged. Giannis has 2 years left, eligible for $275M extension Oct. 1.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The Finals are over. The Giannis clock is ticking.

Heat are the frontrunner. Bucks "comfortable" with Miami's offer.

June 23 draft = deadline. 9 days. #Giannis #NBA #Bucks

facebook.long_form:
The Knicks are champions. The Spurs are going home. And now — the NBA's hottest trade story has no more reason to wait.

Giannis Antetokounmpo's future will be settled before the June 23 NBA Draft. The Bucks wanted to let the Finals play out first, and now the countdown is real: 9 days.

The Miami Heat remain the frontrunner per insiders Marc Stein and Jake Fischer. Giannis's preference reportedly has Miami at the top of his list. The proposed package — Tyler Herro, Kel'el Ware, Nikola Jovic, plus two future first-round picks — is one the Bucks are reportedly "comfortable" with, though nothing is finalized.

The Celtics, Timberwolves, and Magic are also in the picture. Giannis has two years left on his contract and becomes eligible for a four-year, $275 million supermax extension on October 1. The clock is running.

**Where do you think Giannis is headed? Heat? Celtics? Somewhere else?**
facebook.image_caption: Giannis trade countdown: 9 days to the June 23 deadline. Heat leading. What happens next? #Giannis #NBA

article.headline: Giannis Trade Countdown Begins: Heat Still Frontrunner as Bucks Set June 23 Deadline

stories.title: NBA Draft 9 Days Away — Dybantsa Makes Playoff Promise to Wizards
stories.angle: AJ Dybantsa visited Washington Wizards pre-draft and said: "If I go to DC, I'll make the playoffs — I'm gonna make sure I make it." BYU star averaged 25.5 PPG / 51.0% FG. Wizards hold No. 1 pick (17-65, 14.0% lottery odds). First Washington No. 1 since John Wall 2010. Draft is June 23-24 at Barclays Center, Brooklyn — 9 days away. Darryn Peterson still in No. 1 conversation.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
AJ Dybantsa to the Wizards: "If I go to DC, I'll make the playoffs. I'm gonna make sure I make it."

Bold promise. No. 1 prospect. 9 days until the 2026 NBA Draft. #NBADraft #Wizards

facebook.long_form:
Nine days until the 2026 NBA Draft. And the No. 1 prospect just made a promise to the team that plans to pick him.

AJ Dybantsa visited the Washington Wizards ahead of the June 23 draft at Barclays Center in Brooklyn and did not mince words.

"If I go to DC, I'll make the playoffs," Dybantsa said. "I'm gonna make sure I make it."

The former BYU star averaged 25.5 points and shot 51.0% from the field in his one college season. At 6-foot-9 and 210 pounds, he projects as a franchise forward. The Wizards hold the No. 1 pick after finishing 17-65 — their first top selection since drafting John Wall in 2010.

Dybantsa knows what he is walking into in Washington. He is promising to change it. The draft and the Giannis trade deadline fall on the same night — June 23 at Barclays is going to be quite a scene.

**How soon do you think Dybantsa gets the Wizards to the playoffs?**
facebook.image_caption: 9 days. 1 bold promise from AJ Dybantsa. The 2026 NBA Draft is almost here. #NBADraft #Wizards

article.headline: Dybantsa's Playoff Promise: AJ Makes Bold Vow to Wizards With Draft 9 Days Away
